sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचभूय एव महाबाहो श्रृणु मे परमं वचः । यत्तेऽहं प्रीयमाणाय वक्ष्यामि हितकाम्यया ॥
hindiTranslation: श्रीभगवान् बोले  हे महाबाहो अर्जुन  मेरे परम वचनको तुम फिर भी सुनो, जिसे मैं तुम्हारे हितकी कामनासे कहूँगा क्योंकि तुम मेरेमें अत्यन्त प्रेम रखते हो ॥
englishTranslation: The Blessed Lord said: Listen again, O mighty-armed one, to my supreme word, which I will speak for your welfare because you are deeply dear to me.
essence: Deeper truth arrives as care for the one who is ready.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna asks Arjuna to listen again to a deeper teaching, spoken for Arjuna’s good because he is dear to him.
simpleInsight: Being loved means being taught what you most need, not only what you want to hear.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra battlefield, Arjuna stands overwhelmed while Krishna prepares to reveal a deeper teaching. This verse opens the tenth chapter by showing that the coming wisdom is not abstract; it is spoken personally, out of care, to steady Arjuna before the war.
modernRelevance: A mentor leans in after your second failed attempt and says, “Listen carefully — I’m telling you this for your good.” The hardest truths often arrive wrapped in care.
translationLiteral: श्रीभगवानुवाच (the Blessed Lord said) / भूयः (again) / एव (indeed) / महाबाहो (O mighty-armed one) / श्रृणु (listen) / मे (to my) / परमम् (supreme) / वचः (word) / यत् (which) / ते (to you) / अहम् (I) / प्रीयमाणाय (to one who is dear) / वक्ष्यामि (will speak) / हितकाम्यया (with desire for your welfare)
